pysoniq.gain module¶

Gain and volume control

pysoniq.gain.adjust_gain_level(audio, gain=None)[source]¶

Adjust audio gain level

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array

  • gain – float, if None uses global gain (1.0 = unity, <1.0 = reduce, >1.0 = boost)

Returns:

audio with gain adjusted (clipped to -1, 1)

pysoniq.gain.compress(audio, threshold_db=-20.0, ratio=4.0, attack_ms=5.0, release_ms=50.0, sr=44100)[source]¶

Simple dynamics compression

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array (mono)

  • threshold_db – float, compression threshold

  • ratio – float, compression ratio (e.g., 4.0 = 4:1)

  • attack_ms – float, attack time in milliseconds

  • release_ms – float, release time in milliseconds

  • sr – int, sample rate

Returns:

compressed audio

pysoniq.gain.db_to_linear(db)[source]¶

Convert dB to linear gain

Parameters:

db – float, decibels

Returns:

linear gain

pysoniq.gain.get_gain()[source]¶

Get current global gain (linear)

pysoniq.gain.get_volume_db()[source]¶

Get current global volume in dB

pysoniq.gain.limiter(audio, threshold_db=-1.0, ceiling_db=-0.1)[source]¶

Hard limiter to prevent clipping

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array

  • threshold_db – float, where limiting starts

  • ceiling_db – float, absolute maximum level

Returns:

limited audio

pysoniq.gain.linear_to_db(gain)[source]¶

Convert linear gain to dB

Parameters:

gain – float, linear gain

Returns:

dB value

pysoniq.gain.normalize(audio, target_db=-3.0)[source]¶

Normalize audio to target dB level

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array

  • target_db – float, target peak level in dB (e.g., -3.0)

Returns:

normalized audio

pysoniq.gain.set_gain(gain)[source]¶

Set global gain (linear)

Parameters:

gain – float, 0.0 to 2.0+ (1.0 = unity)

pysoniq.gain.set_volume_db(db)[source]¶

Set global volume in dB

Parameters:

db – float, typically -60 to +6 dB (0 dB = unity)

pysoniq
